name: Gauri
imageAlt: Gauri, a red-and-white Gir cow with curved horns and a calm gaze
story: Our first Gir cow and the matriarch of the herd. She set the standard for what gentleness looks like — and is, to date, our most prolific milker. Every newcomer is introduced to her first.

name: Nandini
imageAlt: Nandini, a light reddish-brown Sahiwal cow with a soft expression
story: Rescued from a tabela in Old Delhi where she had been kept tied for years. Her recovery — physical and emotional — took eighteen months. She now walks free and is our gentlest high-yielder.

name: Lakshmi
imageAlt: Lakshmi, a pure white Tharparkar cow standing in dappled sunlight
story: Tharparkar through and through — desert-born, drought-tolerant, almost stoic. She arrived from a Rajasthan rescue weighing too little and now leads the herd to grazing every dawn.

name: Saraswati
imageAlt: Saraswati, a greyish-white Bachaur cow with a small bell and quiet eyes
story: The Bachaur breed — quiet, compact, deeply rooted in Bihar's farming history. Saraswati was the first cow we ever bought, with a loan from a temple trust. Her gentleness is the standard we hold every newcomer to.

name: Kamadhenu
imageAlt: Kamadhenu, a small Punganur dwarf cow with delicate horns and a wreath of marigolds
story: Our Punganur. One of the smallest cattle breeds in the world — barely waist-high — and considered sacred in temple traditions across South India. Named for the celestial wish-cow of legend. She produces a small amount of extraordinarily rich, high-butterfat milk, and every child who visits asks to feed her first.

name: Radha
imageAlt: Radha, a young red Gir cow with playful eyes and a still-soft hump
story: Our youngest, born here on a monsoon morning to a mother we lost the same day. Hand-raised by the team. She greets every visitor by name (or so it feels) and has a particular fondness for children.
